records.ety: [L. conferre to bring together, contribute, consult; con- + ferre to bear: cf. F. confrer. See 1st Bear.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conferred (#); p. pr. & vb. n. Conferring.]

defs: To bring together for comparison; to compare.
quotes: If we confer these observations with others of the like nature, we may find cause to rectify the general opinion.

defs: To grant as a possession; to bestow.
quotes: The public marks of honor and reward Conferred upon me.
The closeness and compactness of the parts resting together doth much confer to the strength of the union.

defs: To have discourse; to consult; to compare views; to deliberate.
quotes: Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered.
You shall hear us confer of this.
